photograph detail of some petals on mealy cup Sage including at near center what looks to my eye like purple pollen, there's also dried leaves which show the deep blue color which otherwise looks more like purple because it is diffused with light.The plant in the image is Mealycup Sage (Salvia farinacea), specifically a variety like 'Victoria Blue'. 
Flower Color: Deep violet-blue flowers with white, mealy-textured stems.
Growing Habit: Shrubby, clump-forming tender perennial that grows 1.5–3 feet tall.
Pollinator Friendly: Highly attractive to butterflies, bees, and hummingbirds.
Cultivation: Hardy in USDA Zones 8-10, thrives in full sun with average, well-drained soil.

Opening Day! (my favorite time of the year)

images.alt:
Opening Day marks the beginning of the Major League Baseball season, a tradition celebrated by fans across the United States and beyond. Often referred to as baseball’s true first day of spring, it symbolizes renewal, optimism, and the return of the game after the long offseason.

The tradition of Opening Day dates back to the 19th century, with the first official professional baseball game played in 1871. Over time, it has grown into a ceremonial event, often featuring packed stadiums, pregame festivities, and a sense of excitement shared by fans, players, and teams alike.

A special connection exists between Opening Day and the Cincinnati Reds, who historically hosted the first game of the season due to their status as baseball’s first professional team. While scheduling has changed in modern times, the spirit of that tradition still lingers.

Opening Day is more than just the start of games—it represents hope for every team, a fresh record, and the belief that anything is possible in the season ahead. For many fans, it is one of the most anticipated days of the year, signaling the return of summer nights, ballpark food, and the timeless rhythm of baseball.

images.alt: A cinematic, ultra-realistic portrait of a beautiful female gunslinger inspired by Django-style Western themes, standing in a dusty Old West town. She wears a weathered leather duster coat, a fitted corset vest, a wide-brim cowboy hat, and a red bandana around her neck. She holds two revolvers confidently, one aimed forward toward the viewer and the other lowered at her side. Her expression is intense, fearless, and determined. The background shows wooden saloons, a water tower, and a hazy desert atmosphere with warm golden sunlight and drifting dust particles. Highly detailed textures on clothing and skin, dramatic lighting, shallow depth of field, cinematic composition, 4K, sharp focus.
